neymar_and_brazil_uncertainty.note: Brazil candidates carry brazil_neymar_usage_source_gap when inherited from score/projection context. Neymar itself remains heavily penalized and should not be promoted without source-backed usage.

notes: Safe can overlap with Balanced because high starts, minutes, and floor are also good overall signals.
Upside can overlap with Captain Alpha, but it should not be a duplicate armband list.
Differential should separate from Balanced/Safe by penalizing obvious top-list players while retaining projection defensibility.

v3_vs_v2_unique_player_comparison.interpretation: Changes are mainly driven by official scoring, official prices/positions, the fantasy-pool minutes model, and score predictor v3. Remaining suspicious changes are tied to projection categories that are not yet fully modeled.

checks.details: All five staged modes produced candidate rows.
All candidate rows have required fields.
233 blocked players remain outside candidate lists.
Recommendation scores, points, start probabilities, and minutes are numeric and in range.
Candidate rows carry fantasy_pool_only and not_final_squad_backed flags.
Balanced top 25 has no low-confidence, thin-profile, or true missing-usage candidates.
Captain Alpha top 25 is not dominated by defenders or goalkeepers.
Safe mode top 25 is not dominated by goalkeepers.
Safe mode top 25 keeps high/medium confidence, acceptable starts, and acceptable minutes.
Safe mode top 25 avoids high downside, high volatility, and poor role-stability finance rows.
Differential mode top 25 avoids weak low-minute, low-projection players.
Differential mode top 25 has bridge-backed finance context, acceptable finance alpha, downside, and role stability.
Candidate rows carry bridge-aware fantasy-pool finance v2 context.
High finance-alpha rows in candidate lists are not low-start rows.
High finance-alpha rows in candidate lists are not high-downside rows.
Differential top candidate is not the same candidate row as Balanced top candidate.
Differential top 10 is not dominated by Balanced top 10 candidate rows.
Upside top 10 overlaps Captain Alpha by 0 rows; top 25 overlap is 0.
Upside mode top 25 includes credible attacking players.

Mode-specific position caps and score adjustments are recorded in QA and the calibration audit. Global top-25 views may still concentrate when the same high-scoring player appears across scopes, but per-scope lists obey caps.
Neymar and Brazil uncertainty flags are preserved from the projection layer.
This script writes only staged data/matchdayRecommendations_fantasyPool_v3.json, QA JSON, and QA report.
Outputs are marked unsafe for final public recommendations and Team Builder promotion.

stop_conditions.details: No source-backed final squad rows are available; recommendation candidates remain fantasy_pool_only.
Official rules still carry manual-review warnings, including unresolved booster/deadline semantics.
Player projection v3 is staged and blocked from final promotion.
Score predictor v3 is fantasy-pool-only and not final-squad-backed.
This script intentionally does not update browser-ready recommendation files.
Active v2 recommendation files are preserved; v3 candidates are separate staged outputs.
Neymar remains a P0 national-team usage source gap; Brazil context keeps uncertainty flags.

warnings: All recommendation candidates are fantasy_pool_only and not final-squad-backed.
Official rules still require manual review.
Neymar remains a P0 national-team usage source gap.
These outputs are not browser-ready and do not update active recommendation files.
recommended_next_step: Review staged recommendation candidates, then build a preliminary QA review workflow or score predictor/projection comparison pass. Do not promote to public recommendations until final squads and rules blockers are resolved.
